content.full_text:
In kind contributions to
post election statements/cumulation
82015

A-76-593

March 3, 1976


Floyd S. Mori
CapitalMall
Sacramento CA 95814

Dear Assemblyman Mori:

Enclosed please find a copy of the staff's most recent legislative
package. As I indicated to you last wednesday, the Commission, in its
meeting of March 2-4, will be considering which of these staff pro-
posals it wishes the legislature to enact. If you have any comments or
suggestions, please feel free to contact me.

With respect to the reporting of private airplane flights, the
staff concluded that such services may be reported as in-kind contribu-
tions to you or your committee. These flights need not be reported on
your statement of economic interests so long as they are connected with
your role as a legislator or politician. For a more thorough discussion of
the reporting requirements, I am enclosing a copy of the opinion requested
by Assemblyman Willie Brown (75-605). As to the value of such flights, you
should make a good faith estimate based on any information provided by a
donor. I staff thought that the price of a commercial airline ticket
would not be an appropriate measure of value in most instances.

Staff also concluded that your interpretation of the cumulation pro-
visions of Chapter 4 was correct, i.e., that cumulation must occur from
one post-election statement to the next without respect to whether the
elections are state or local.

I enjoyed meeting with you last week and appreciate your offer
to help us implement to the Act which further its purposes. If you
have any further comments or questions, please give me a call.

Sincerely,

Ted Fris
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

TP:pvp

sections.question_synthetic: The document discusses reporting requirements for private airplane flights as in-kind contributions and the interpretation of cumulation provisions under Chapter 4, but does not present a formal legal question.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is correspondence rather than formal advice letter format. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Contains substantive legal guidance on airplane flight reporting and cumulation provisions but delivered in informal letter format. References Assemblyman Willie Brown opinion 75-055 for more thorough discussion.

Mori CapitalMall Sacramento CA 95814 Dear Assemblyman Mori: Enclosed please find a copy of the staff's most recent legislative package. As I indicated to you last wednesday, the Commission, in its meeting of March 2-4, will be considering which of these staff pro- posals it wishes the legislature to enact. If you have any comments or suggestions, please feel free to contact me. With respect to the reporting of private airplane flights, the staff concluded that such services may be reported as in-kind contribu- tions to you or your committee. These flights need not be reported on your statement of economic interests so long as they are connected with your role as a legislator or politician. For a more thorough discussion of the reporting requirements, I am enclosing a copy of the opinion requested by Assemblyman Willie Brown (75-605). As to the value of such flights, you should make a good faith estimate based on any information provided by a donor. I staff thought that the price of a commercial airline ticket would not be an appropriate measure of value in most instances. Staff also concluded that your interpretation of the cumulation pro- visions of Chapter 4 was correct, i.e., that cumulation must occur from one post-election statement to the next without respect to whether the elections are state or local. Sincerely, Ted Fris Staff Counsel Legal Division TP:pvp
embedding.summary: This is an informal letter from FPPC Staff Counsel Ted Prin to Assemblyman Floyd Mori discussing the staff's legislative package and providing guidance on two matters: (1) private airplane flights may be reported as in-kind contributions and need not be reported on statements of economic interests if connected to legislative/political role, with value estimated in good faith; and (2) confirmation that Mori's interpretation of cumulation provisions under Chapter 4 is correct.
